experiment: M6 multi-reference-frame LLM readout (frozen Qwen2.5-1.5B reads grid + object-vector code, answers allocentric WHERE and egocentric LANDMARK)

where._reads: grid (allocentric) -- which of 9 room cells, 9 classes

landmark._reads: object-vector cells (egocentric) -- which of 8 sectors the landmark is in, 8 classes

organ_specificity: clean DOUBLE dissociation at the language level: WHERE (allocentric) collapses only when the grid is ablated (9% vs 53%); LANDMARK (egocentric) collapses only when the object-vector cells are ablated (10% vs 35%) -- the LLM reads each reference frame specifically from its own organ.
honest_notes: Both frames read above chance and the deltas are large (WHERE +42, LANDMARK +26), but neither ON-vs-OFF clears p<0.05 at n=6 (p=0.094, 0.123) -- wide cross-seed magnitude variance, as in m5/torus/elapsed-time readouts.
The decisive evidence is the organ-specific DOUBLE dissociation (each frame dies only when its own organ is ablated), which is a within-condition contrast and cleaner than the ON/OFF p-values.
Direction is consistent; the open item is reaching ON-vs-OFF significance (n>=8 or readout-stability tuning), as for the other single-item readouts.
